steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage has two time frames: 1940, when scientists first isolated androstenone and assumed it existed only in animals; and a later point when they discovered it also appears in chokecherry plants. The blank opens the second sentence, so the transition must signal a shift from past to present while acknowledging that the later discovery changed what scientists know.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Today,’: “Today” marks a temporal shift from the 1940 past to the present moment, directly contrasting the earlier assumption with current knowledge. The sentence then adds “but this discovery didn’t occur until decades later,” which explains how we got from 1940 to today. This is the only choice that names a new time frame. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘For instance,’: “For instance” signals an example of what was just said. The second sentence is not an example of scientists assuming androstenone exists only in animals; it contradicts that assumption. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘In other words,’: “In other words” signals a restatement. The second sentence introduces new information (plant sources) rather than rephrasing the idea that androstenone was isolated in 1940. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Thus,’: “Thus” signals a conclusion caused by what preceded it. Current knowledge about plant androstenone is not a logical result of the 1940 isolation; it is a separate later discovery. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
